growth_records.medium: PETC medium (gas-to-liquid ratio 3.0; 40 mL culture volume)

up.method: GC headspace gas analysis; Delta(headspace conc) x headspace vol / culture vol

growth_records.cite: Joungmin Lee et al. Domestication of the novel alcohologenic acetogen Clostridium sp. AWRP: from isolation to characterization for syngas fermentation. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2019). DOI:10.1186/s13068-019-1570-0. [PMC6757427]
growth_records.snippet: CO consumed 157 +/- 1; H2 consumed 29 +/- 2; CO2 produced 52 +/- 2; Acetate 28 +/- 2; Ethanol 8 +/- 1; 2,3-Butanediol ND; C-recovery 79 +/- 1% (Table 2, gas-to-liquid ratio 3.0)
growth_records.notes: Table 2, PETC medium, gas-to-liquid ratio 3.0 (40 mL culture). Values are cumulative consumed/produced amounts normalized to culture volume, not per-time rates; time and biomass not paired so mmol/gDW/h not derivable for this batch condition. Optimum growth 37 C, pH 6.0-6.5 (isolation section). Syngas blend 50% CO/10% H2/10% CO2/30% N2. DCW correlation given: 0.27 g DCW per OD600 unit.

growth_records.medium: PETC medium (gas-to-liquid ratio 6.9; 20 mL culture volume)
growth_records.cite: Joungmin Lee et al. Domestication of the novel alcohologenic acetogen Clostridium sp. AWRP: from isolation to characterization for syngas fermentation. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2019). DOI:10.1186/s13068-019-1570-0. [PMC6757427]
growth_records.snippet: CO consumed 368 +/- 2; H2 consumed 15 +/- 5; CO2 produced 186 +/- 11; Acetate 40 +/- 4; Ethanol 24 +/- 2; 2,3-Butanediol 2 +/- 0; C-recovery 87 +/- 2% (Table 2, gas-to-liquid ratio 6.9)
growth_records.notes: Table 2, PETC medium, gas-to-liquid ratio 6.9 (20 mL culture). Cumulative amounts normalized to culture volume; not per-time rates.

growth_records.medium: PETC medium (gas-to-liquid ratio 14.8; 10 mL culture volume)
growth_records.cite: Joungmin Lee et al. Domestication of the novel alcohologenic acetogen Clostridium sp. AWRP: from isolation to characterization for syngas fermentation. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2019). DOI:10.1186/s13068-019-1570-0. [PMC6757427]
growth_records.snippet: CO consumed 778 +/- 7; H2 consumed 7 +/- 3; CO2 produced 434 +/- 3; Acetate 14 +/- 2; Ethanol 94 +/- 0; 2,3-Butanediol 6 +/- 1; C-recovery 87 +/- 0% (Table 2, gas-to-liquid ratio 14.8)
growth_records.notes: Table 2, PETC medium, gas-to-liquid ratio 14.8 (10 mL culture, highest gas supply). Highest ethanol (94 mM) and 2,3-BDO (6 mM) titers; alcohol favored over acetate at high gas-to-liquid ratio. Cumulative amounts normalized to culture volume; not per-time rates.

growth_records.medium: RM medium (bioreactor batch syngas fermentation; +0.5 g/L yeast extract, +50 mg/L L-methionine)

up.method: syngas fed continuously; per-time uptake not quantified in bioreactor run
syngas fed continuously; not quantified per time

sec.method: reported specific production 191-217 mM (g DCW)^-1 day^-1 (Phase I, 30-49 h); converted 217/24
reported 170 mM (g DCW)^-1 day^-1 (Phase III, 85.3-96 h); converted 170/24
reported 100 mM (g DCW)^-1 day^-1 (Phase II, 57.5-85.3 h); converted 100/24

growth_records.cite: Joungmin Lee et al. Domestication of the novel alcohologenic acetogen Clostridium sp. AWRP: from isolation to characterization for syngas fermentation. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2019). DOI:10.1186/s13068-019-1570-0. [PMC6757427]
growth_records.snippet: specific production rate of ethanol was 191-217 mM (g DCW)-1 day-1 between 30 and 49 h... specific growth rate of 0.8 day-1 (30-35 h) that increased to 1.4 day-1 (35-49 h), reaching 0.37 g DCW L-1... final titers of ethanol, 2,3-butanediol,
growth_records.notes: Batch bioreactor (RM medium, 1.6 L). growth_rate: reported 0.8 day-1 (30-35 h) and 1.4 day-1 (35-49 h); converted mu=1.4/24=0.0583 h-1 (peak), doubling_time=ln2/0.0583=11.9 h; early 0.8/day=0.0333 h-1 (td 20.8 h). Ethanol specific production 191-217 mM(gDCW)^-1 day^-1 (Phase I) => 191/24=7.96 to 217/24=9.04 mmol/gDW/h; Phase III 170/24=7.08. Acetate 100/24=4.17 mmol/gDW/h (Phase II). pH held 5.0-5.8 (midpoint 5.4). Max cell mass 0.37 g DCW/L. CO/H2 uptake not quantified per time. Paper also states specific ethanol production in Phases I and III were 134-217 and 41-172 mM(gDCW)^-1 day^-1 over full phase windows.

growth_records.cite: Joungmin Lee et al. Domestication of the novel alcohologenic acetogen Clostridium sp. AWRP: from isolation to characterization for syngas fermentation. Biotechnology for Biofuels (2019). DOI:10.1186/s13068-019-1570-0. [PMC6757427]
growth_records.snippet: The final ethanol titer was the highest in the PETC medium (80 mM). In the RM medium, strain AWRP produced slightly less ethanol (70 mM)... Small amounts of 2,3-butanediol were detected in both media as well (PETC, 5 mM; RM, 2 mM).
growth_records.notes: Fig 4 medium comparison. RM serum-bottle: ethanol 70 mM, 2,3-BDO 2 mM, ~7 days. PETC serum-bottle gave ethanol 80 mM, 2,3-BDO 5 mM (~12 days). AM medium supported poorest growth. Titers only; no uptake or per-time rate. Distinct medium/condition from Table 2 PETC records.

growth_records.cite: Rastogi G et al. Presence of glucose, xylose, and glycerol fermenting bacteria in the deep biosphere of the former Homestake gold mine, South Dakota. Frontiers in Microbiology (2013). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2013.00018. [PMC3573265]
growth_records.snippet: ethanol (0.205–0.304 g ethanol/g glucose) was detected as the primary product ... Specific growth rate μ(h−1) 0.030 ... pH 10.0 − 4.76
growth_records.notes: Clostridium sp. isolate. μ=0.030 h⁻¹ (Table1). doubling_time = ln2/0.030 = 23.1 h. Ethanol conc derived = 0.205 g/g × 5 g/L = 1.03 g/L. Yxs=0.006 g/g (Table2, ethanol yield 0.205). Lactic acid dropped below detection at end. Biomass measured as total cell protein.

up.units: g/L/h (derived: 5 g/L fully consumed within 40 h = 5/40)

growth_records.cite: Rastogi G et al. Presence of glucose, xylose, and glycerol fermenting bacteria in the deep biosphere of the former Homestake gold mine, South Dakota. Frontiers in Microbiology (2013). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2013.00018. [PMC3573265]
growth_records.snippet: within 40 h of fermentation, DUSELGlu4 completely utilized the glucose ... Specific growth rate μ(h−1) 0.038 ... ethanol yield 0.293 ... pH 10.0 − 4.65
growth_records.notes: Clostridium sp. isolate; highest glucose μ. μ=0.038 h⁻¹ (Table1). doubling_time=ln2/0.038=18.2 h. Glucose 5 g/L fully consumed within 40 h → volumetric uptake 5/40 = 0.125 g/L/h (biomass-specific rate not computable: biomass reported as cell protein, no gDW). Ethanol conc derived = 0.293 g/g × 5 g/L = 1.465 g/L. Yxs=0.008 g/g.

up.units: g/L/h (derived: 43% of 5 g/L = 2.15 g/L consumed over 150 h)

growth_records.cite: Rastogi G et al. Presence of glucose, xylose, and glycerol fermenting bacteria in the deep biosphere of the former Homestake gold mine, South Dakota. Frontiers in Microbiology (2013). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2013.00018. [PMC3573265]
growth_records.snippet: DUSELXyl3 produced acetic acid (1.56 g/L) as a primary metabolite rather than ethanol (0.52 g/L) ... 0.189 g of ethanol/g of xylose ... this strain fermented only about 43% of the initial xylose concentration during 150 h
growth_records.notes: Clostridium sp. Xylose fermenter; acetic acid is major product (yield 0.585 g/g from abstract), ethanol minor (yield 0.189 g/g). μ=0.025 h⁻¹ (Table1). doubling_time=ln2/0.025=27.7 h. Xylose uptake volumetric: 43% × 5 g/L = 2.15 g/L over 150 h → 2.15/150 = 0.0143 g/L/h. Measured concs: acetic acid 1.56 g/L, ethanol 0.52 g/L. pH 10.0→4.5. Yxs=0.006 g/g. Biomass = total cell protein.

growth_records.medium: Glycerol fermentation medium (pH 7; from Zheng et al. 2008)

up.units: g/L/h (derived: ~10% of 40 g/L = 4 g/L consumed over 66 h)

sec.units: g/L (1.44-1.64 g/L range across the three glycerol strains)
g/L (1.18-1.27 g/L range across the three glycerol strains)
below detection limit at end of fermentation (minor, transient)

growth_records.cite: Rastogi G et al. Presence of glucose, xylose, and glycerol fermenting bacteria in the deep biosphere of the former Homestake gold mine, South Dakota. Frontiers in Microbiology (2013). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2013.00018. [PMC3573265]
growth_records.snippet: PDO (1.44–1.64 g/L) and ethanol (1.18–1.27 g/L) as the major fermentation products ... only about 10% of the initial glycerol concentration was consumed ... DUSELGly1 ... ethanol yield 0.284 ... PDO yield 0.323
growth_records.notes: Clostridium sp. glycerol fermenter; PDO growth-associated. μ=0.031 h⁻¹ (Table1). doubling_time=ln2/0.031=22.4 h. Glycerol uptake volumetric: ~10% × 40 g/L = 4 g/L over 66 h → 4/66 = 0.0606 g/L/h. Product concs (1.44-1.64 g/L PDO; 1.18-1.27 g/L ethanol) reported as ranges for the 3 glycerol strains collectively; acetic acid 0.09 g/L. Yields (Table2): PDO 0.323 g/g, ethanol 0.284 g/g, Yxs 0.007. Glycerol medium adapted from Zheng et al. 2008. pH 7.0→5.22.

growth_records.medium: Glycerol fermentation medium (pH 7; from Zheng et al. 2008)

up.units: g/L/h (derived: ~10% of 40 g/L = 4 g/L over 66 h)

sec.units: g/L (1.44-1.64 g/L range across the three glycerol strains)
g/L (1.18-1.27 g/L range across the three glycerol strains)

growth_records.cite: Rastogi G et al. Presence of glucose, xylose, and glycerol fermenting bacteria in the deep biosphere of the former Homestake gold mine, South Dakota. Frontiers in Microbiology (2013). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2013.00018. [PMC3573265]
growth_records.snippet: DUSELGly3 ... Glycerol ... PDO and Ethanol ... ethanol yield 0.298 ... PDO yield 0.367 ... Specific growth rate μ(h−1) 0.037 ... pH 7.0 − 5.32
growth_records.notes: Clostridium sp. glycerol fermenter. μ=0.037 h⁻¹ (Table1). doubling_time=ln2/0.037=18.7 h. Glycerol uptake volumetric ~4 g/L over 66 h = 0.0606 g/L/h. Yields (Table2): PDO 0.367 g/g, ethanol 0.298 g/g, Yxs 0.007. Product concentration ranges shared across 3 glycerol strains. pH 7.0→5.32.

growth_records.medium: Glycerol fermentation medium (pH 7; from Zheng et al. 2008)

up.units: g/L/h (derived: ~10% of 40 g/L = 4 g/L over 66 h)

sec.units: g/L (upper end of 1.44-1.64 g/L range; DUSELGly4 highest PDO yield)

growth_records.cite: Rastogi G et al. Presence of glucose, xylose, and glycerol fermenting bacteria in the deep biosphere of the former Homestake gold mine, South Dakota. Frontiers in Microbiology (2013). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2013.00018. [PMC3573265]
growth_records.snippet: DUSELGly4 demonstrated a higher specific growth rate of 0.048 h−1, ethanol yield of 0.35 g/g, and PDO yield of 0.458 g/g ... The PDO yield of DUSELGly4 (0.458 g PDO/g of glycerol) was comparable to K. pneumoniae M5al 0.41 g/g
growth_records.notes: Clostridium sp.; best glycerol fermenter. μ=0.048 h⁻¹ (Table1). doubling_time=ln2/0.048=14.4 h. Glycerol uptake volumetric ~4 g/L over 66 h = 0.0606 g/L/h. Highest yields (Table2): PDO 0.458 g/g, ethanol 0.350 g/g; assigned upper-end product concs (PDO 1.64, ethanol 1.27 g/L) of the reported 1.44-1.64 / 1.18-1.27 g/L ranges as DUSELGly4 had highest yields. Yxs 0.007. pH 7.0→5.22.
